seoTitle: How to Alphabetize a List in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Sort text lists alphabetically in Google Docs using a free sorting add-on, since there's no built-in A-Z button in the native toolbar.
intro: Google Docs doesn't have a native alphabetize button, but a free add-on can sort your lists in seconds. This guide covers installing a sorting extension and using it to arrange text alphabetically without manually retyping anything.

steps.heading: Open the add-ons marketplace
steps.body: Click Extensions in the top menu, then select Get Add-ons to browse available tools.

steps.heading: Install a sorting add-on
steps.body: Search for a tool like DocTools or Sorted Paragraphs, select the verified listing, and click Install.

steps.heading: Highlight your list
steps.body: Select the full text list you want sorted, making sure not to include unrelated paragraphs.

steps.heading: Run the sort command
steps.body: Click Extensions, hover over the installed add-on, and choose 'Sort Ascending (A-Z)' or the equivalent descending option.

steps.heading: Review the sorted result
steps.body: Check the reorganized list for stray blank lines or formatting issues before finalizing the document.

faq.question: Can I sort a table column instead of a plain list?
faq.answer: Some add-ons support column-specific sorting inside tables, though features vary by extension.

faq.question: Is there a way to alphabetize without installing an add-on?
faq.answer: You can copy the list into Google Sheets, use its SORT function, then paste it back into Docs.

faq.question: Will sorting affect any formatting like bold or italics?
faq.answer: Most add-ons preserve basic formatting, but it's worth double-checking after a sort.

faq.question: Can I sort in descending order too?
faq.answer: Yes, most sorting add-ons offer both A-Z and Z-A options.
